seed_problem: Facility placement on a weighted grid with every demand point routed to an opened facility; feasibility is coverage by geometric reach and a valid canal path, while cost combines opening, distance, and convex edge congestion.
mutation: composite: fuse capacitated-looking set cover, explicit routed flow on a canal grid, and squared Manhattan reach geometry. Cheap high-radius pumps can cover many basins but route all water through narrow canals; stronger solutions open a spread of district pumps and use congestion-aware detours.
theme: flood-barrier pump placement on a canal grid
title: Canal Floodwall Pump Siting
strategies: all-open emergency baseline: open every pump and assign each basin to its co-located radius-0 emergency pump
coverage-per-cost greedy: open cheap high-reach pumps that cover the most still-uncovered basins, then route by shortest paths
spread placement: open the non-emergency district pumps instead of relying on a single central trap
congestion-aware routing: route heavy basins first with incremental convex edge-load costs so flow avoids narrow canals and relief routes are used
